seoTitle: How to Buy Coursera Plus: Plans and Pricing Guide
metaDescription: Walk through buying Coursera Plus step by step, from comparing plans and pricing to completing checkout for unlimited access to thousands of courses.
intro: Coursera Plus gives you unlimited access to thousands of courses for one subscription price, but picking the right plan and getting through checkout isn't always obvious the first time. This covers how to compare the available plans, sign up, and start using your subscription right away.

steps.heading: Review the available Coursera Plus plans
steps.body: Compare the pricing options Coursera offers so you can pick the plan that matches how much you plan to learn.

steps.heading: Navigate to the Coursera Plus signup page
steps.body: From the Coursera platform, find the Coursera Plus offer and start the signup process.

steps.heading: Select your plan and enter payment details
steps.body: Choose the plan that fits your budget and timeline, then complete the payment step to activate your subscription.

steps.heading: Start browsing your unlimited course access
steps.body: Once your purchase goes through, you can immediately start enrolling in any of the courses included with Coursera Plus.

faq.question: What's included with a Coursera Plus subscription?
faq.answer: It gives you unlimited access to the courses covered under the plan, across a wide range of subjects and industries.

faq.question: Are there different pricing tiers to choose from?
faq.answer: Yes, Coursera offers multiple plan and pricing options, so it's worth comparing them before you commit.

faq.question: Is Coursera Plus worth it for someone taking just one course?
faq.answer: If you only need a single course, a one-off purchase may make more sense than a subscription, but Coursera Plus pays off if you plan to take several.

faq.question: Can I use Coursera Plus for career-focused learning?
faq.answer: Yes, the subscription is commonly used by professionals and students looking to build skills across business, technology, and other in-demand areas.
